data.title: Why Is Penny Stock Beyond Meat (BYND) Up Over 8% Today?
data.description:
Beyond Meat (BYND), a penny stock, moved over 8% in Thursday's trading after the plant-based food maker announced plans to enter the beverage space with a new protein-infused sparkling drink. The price hike reflects that investors appear encouraged by Beyond Meat's willingness to explore new categories as its core business remains under pressure.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In its most recent quarterly results, Beyond Meat reported a sharp drop in revenue, underlining the need for new growth drivers. Sales fell 13.3% year over year to $70.22 million, while earnings per share came in at -$0.47, missing analysts' consensus estimate of -$0.40.

Beyond Meat Tests a New Growth Idea

Beyond Meat is launching Beyond Immerse, a flavored sparkling beverage made with plant-based protein. The drinks offer either 10 grams or 20 grams of protein, along with fiber, antioxidants, and electrolytes. The company is pitching the product as a lighter and easier option than heavy protein shakes, aimed at consumers who want protein without a thick texture or strong taste.

The drinks will be sold for a limited time starting January 15 through Beyond Meat's Beyond Test Kitchen platform. The lineup includes three flavors: peach mango, lemon lime, and orange tangerine. A limited release allows the company to test demand before committing to a wider launch.

Is BYND Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Sell consensus rating on BYND stock based on one Hold and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average BYND price target of $0.93 per share implies 10.26% downside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: U.S. Futures Rise as TSMC, NVDA, and Bank Stocks Drive Gains
data.description:
U.S. stock futures moved higher late Thursday as gains in banks and chip stocks lifted market mood. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.25%, 0.06%, and 0.15%, respectively, at 9:12 p.m. EDT on January 15.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In yesterday's trading session, U.S. stocks finished higher across the board. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both rose close to 0.3%, while the Dow gained a stronger 0.6%. Small-cap stocks led the move, with the Russell 2000 climbing nearly 0.9%.

Chip stocks stood out after Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) posted strong fourth-quarter results. The upbeat report revived hopes around the AI trade. Taiwan Semi shares jumped more than 4%, while Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) each gained around 2%.

Banks also helped push the market higher. Goldman Sachs (GS) and Morgan Stanley (MS) both reported solid quarterly results. Goldman shares rose more than 4%, while Morgan Stanley climbed nearly 6%.

Even with Thursday's gains, investors are heading into the end of a busy week on a cautious note. The major indexes are still on track for weekly losses, with the S&P 500 down about 0.3%, the Nasdaq off 0.6%, and the Dow lower by roughly 0.1% so far this week.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Investor Praises Optimus V3 Robot – 'Nobody Will Remember that Tesla Ever Made a Car'
data.description:
Angel investor Jason Calacanis recently stated that after seeing EV maker Tesla's (TSLA) Optimus V3 robot, people may one day forget that Tesla ever made cars. Speaking about a private visit to Tesla's Optimus lab, he described walking in on a Sunday morning and finding a large team actively working. He said what he saw was so impressive that it completely changed how he thinks about Tesla's future. According to Calacanis, Optimus V3 is not just another product but something that could become far bigger than Tesla's car business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, Calacanis said that Optimus will be able to use advanced AI, similar to large language models, to understand the world and carry out real-world tasks that humans do not want to do. He even suggested that there could eventually be a one-to-one ratio of robots to people, meaning one Optimus for every human. In his view, this would make Optimus the most transformative technology product ever created, which is why he thinks Tesla has already won the race in humanoid robots.

While Calacanis' remarks are clearly his personal opinion, they stand out because he is one of the first non-Tesla insiders to speak publicly about Optimus V3. At the same time, Tesla has kept tight control over what has been shown to the public. Elon Musk has said that Optimus V3 has not yet been officially revealed and that photos and videos online still show earlier versions. However, when Calacanis' comments were shared on X, Musk replied simply with "Probably true," which added even more attention to the robot's potential.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 11.1% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: NVDA, GOOGL, META: AI Spending Forecast to Hit $2.53 Trillion This Year
data.description:
Spending on artificial intelligence (AI) shows no signs of slowing down anytime soon.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to business and technology insights firm Gartner (IT), global AI spending will reach $2.53 trillion in 2026 and climb to $3.33 trillion in 2027 as companies continue to pour money into the revolutionary technology that promises to transform life as we know it.

In a new outlook, Gartner says that the bulk of the spending over the next two years will be focused on AI infrastructure, with hyperscalers such as Google parent Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Amazon (AMZN) expected to invest $1.36 trillion to build the backbone of AI in 2026 and another $1.75 trillion in 2027.

Chipmakers Will Spend Heavily, Too

Chipmakers such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) are also expected to continue spending heavily to create ever more powerful processors capable of running AI workloads and the data centers built by the hyperscalers.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has forecast that the chipmaker will sell $500 billion worth of AI chips and semiconductors this year. AMD CEO Lisa Su has said that the AI data center market alone is likely to be worth $1 trillion to chipmakers by 2030.

Gartner adds that heavy spending is likely to continue in the development of AI software, models, and data science over the next few years.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Nvidia's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 41 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy, one Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the past three months. The average NVDA price target of $262.75 implies 40.57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S.-Taiwan Secure Trade Deal with $500 Billion AI and Chip Investment
data.description:
The U.S. and Taiwan have agreed to a trade deal that will see Taiwanese technology companies invest at least $250 billion into the U.S. for semiconductor, energy, and artificial intelligence operations. Furthermore, Taiwan's government has agreed to provide a minimum of $250 billion in credit guarantees to Taiwanese enterprises for investing in the U.S. semiconductor supply chain.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In exchange, the U.S. will lower the self-governing island's tariff rate to 15% from 20% and exempt its generic pharmaceuticals, the ingredients used in those drugs, aircraft components, and some natural resources from tariffs.

TSM Eyes Arizona Expansion Under U.S.-Taiwan Agreement

In addition, Taiwanese companies that contribute to new U.S. semiconductor capacity "may import up to 2.5 times that planned capacity without paying Section 232 duties during the approved construction period." Taiwanese companies that complete new chip projects in the U.S. will be able to import 1.5 times the capacity of finished facilities.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, who led negotiations on the U.S. side, told CNBC that Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) recently purchased land in Arizona and is considering expanding its presence there as part of the deal.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Most Explosive Proxy Battle In…Entertainment": Warner Bros. Discovery Stock (NASDAQ:WBD) Slips as Paramount Considers Nuclear Option
data.description:
Let no one ever say that the sale of entertainment giant Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) was not, in and of itself, entertaining. We have seen fights, skulduggery, and more tricks than a backyard magic show come out of this one. And Paramount Skydance (PSKY) may be considering one more to ultimately come out on top: a proxy battle. This left Warner shareholders a bit uneasy, and sent shares down fractionally in the closing minutes of Thursday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Such a move is already being called, possibly, "…the most explosive proxy battle in the history of the entertainment business." Just three days ago, Paramount filed suit against Warner, looking for an order that would require the Warner board to show just what went into the decision to go with Netflix (NFLX) despite everything that has happened so far. And Paramount may ultimately take this farther, by using the shares it has to vote for new members of the board later on.

With Warner's annual meeting about to be set—the window would open in about three weeks—Paramount may actually have enough vote control to nominate, and approve, a new slate of directors to open board talks. Paramount could also pull in voters to vote "no" on a potential special meeting to approve the deal before the annual meeting even starts.

Then Came Weiss, and Bikes

Things got unusual in the meantime, though. Weiss Ratings recently released a report suggesting that Warner stock was a bad buy right now. In fact, Weiss Ratings gave Warner stock a D+ rating, pointing out "…sharp declines in earnings growth, revenue growth, and cash inflows." Given that much of Warner's recent share price growth came from a potential merger, this may not be out of line.

But Warner also delivered on the sports front. WBD Sports announced the 2026 Mountain Bike World Series teams, with 40 teams qualifying for the event. Qualifying for the event allows these teams to enter registered riders in every round using the format already established. The teams were decided based on total points earnings and riders on each team as of October 28, 2025. Better yet, the top 10 teams have a two-year license.

Is WBD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on WBD stock based on five Buys and 10 Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 202.32% rally in its share price over the past year, the average WBD price target of $25.61 per share implies 9.92% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: TD Cowen Cuts Strategy Stock (MSTR) Price Target on Lower Bitcoin Yield Outlook
data.description:
TD Cowen analyst Lance Vitanza lowered his price target for Strategy (MSTR) stock to $440 from $500 to reflect a reduced Bitcoin (BTC-USD) yield outlook for 2026. Nonetheless, Vitanza reiterated a Buy rating on MSTR stock, saying, "Strategy remains attractive for those looking to create bitcoin exposure." It is worth noting that the analyst's lowered price target still indicates an attractive upside of more than 150% in Bitcoin.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

TD Cowen Analyst Weighs in on MSTR Stock

Vitanza noted that over the past week (ended January 11), Strategy issued 6,827,695 shares of its common stock and 1,192,262 shares of its variable rate Stretch (STRC) preferred stock, raising about $1.25 billion from these offerings. The company "used all but $0.5 million" of the proceeds to buy 13,627 Bitcoins.

"With the purchases funded primarily from equity issued close to parity, the week's activity created scant BTC Yield; thus the transactions would appear to make sense only if one expects Bitcoin prices to recovery relatively quickly," said Vitanza.

The analyst noted that Strategy has been aggressively taking advantage of what many view as a "temporary" pullback in BTC price. Vitanza now expects MSTR to purchase 155,000 Bitcoins, compared to the prior estimate of 90,000 Bitcoins.

Notably, Vitanza expects Strategy to continue issuing common and preferred equity securities and use the proceeds to buy BTC, as long as the price of the largest cryptocurrency remains depressed. The analyst lowered his price target to reflect a higher equity in the mix, which reduces Bitcoin yield.  

Is MSTR a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Strategy stock based on 11 Buys and two Holds. The average MSTR stock price target of $448.18 indicates 162.2% upside potential from current levels. MSTR stock has risen 12.5% so far in 2026, but has declined about 53% over the past year.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: "A License to Print Money": Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up as Lithium Refinery Goes Live
data.description:
It is no secret that electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) requires lithium to live. Lithium is a key component in electric vehicle batteries, which require a sort of refined lithium that is difficult to come by. But Tesla is currently refining lithium on a large scale, answering its own supply chain question and potentially opening up a new revenue stream. The news was a mild help to shareholders, who sent Tesla shares up fractionally in Thursday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla CEO Elon Musk refers to lithium refining as "…a license to print money." And this is reasonable enough; lithium ore by itself is not vanishingly rare. The problem is that you cannot simply plug a rock into a battery and make it store power. The lithium has to be refined, and this process is actually fairly involved. Thus, refined lithium is often difficult to get and increases prices.

Enter Tesla's lithium refinery in Robstown, Texas, not far from Corpus Christi. The process to get this refinery off the ground was surprisingly swift, going from breaking ground in 2023 to launching a full, integrated startup in the closing days of 2025. Now, the plant is up and running, and represents the "…first spodumene to lithium hydroxide refinery in North America." Better yet, the plant runs without acid, which represents a major potential safety hazard gone.

Something Needs to Happen

While Elon Musk has spent a lot of time promising the world at Tesla, he has recently pulled back on a lot of it, and at a time when Deutsche Bank analysts are calling for Tesla to ramp things up. In fact, Deutsche Bank analysts specifically pointed out that a lot of Tesla's biggest moneymakers are comparatively smaller in scope right now.

A note from Deutsche Bank mentioned, "While the autos business at Tesla may underperform in 2026, we think more attention is directed towards the company's robotaxi expansion and efforts at humanoid development." It also looked for "…the race for autonomy and embodied AI" to ramp up significantly in 2026.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and eight Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 6.13% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $394.12 per share implies 12.74% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Why Nvidia Is Pulling Ahead in the AI Chip Race, Says Top Analyst
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Broadcom (AVGO) are all competing for position in the fast-growing AI chip market. Following updates from CES, top D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria reiterated a Buy rating on Nvidia and explained how its execution, costs, and overall platform strength compare with rivals like AMD and Broadcom.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia Outpaces AMD and Broadcom

At CES, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the company's Vera Rubin platform is now in full production. Analyst Gil Luria noted that the system can cut AI token costs by up to ten times and train advanced models using far fewer GPUs. These gains make Nvidia's hardware more efficient and cheaper to operate, helping customers manage AI costs as workloads grow.

Beyond data centers, Nvidia is also pushing deeper into physical AI, including robotics and self-driving systems. As part of this effort, the company introduced the Alpamayo family of open-source models to speed up work in autonomous driving. Luria said this expansion gives Nvidia an added advantage, while AMD and Broadcom remain more focused on traditional data center and networking products.

While rivals continue to invest heavily in AI chips, Luria believes Nvidia's tight link between hardware, software, and developer tools keeps it ahead of the pack. As a result, he expects Nvidia to stay ahead of AMD and Broadcom for the "foreseeable future."

Which Chip Stock Is the Best Buy Right Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three stocks mentioned above, Nvidia offers the highest upside at about 43% and carries a Strong Buy rating from analysts. AMD follows with about 37% upside, and it also holds a Strong Buy rating.

Broadcom rounds out the group with a Strong Buy consensus as well, though its upside is lower at around 31% based on current analyst price targets.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Climb on U.S.-Taiwan Trade Deal; Easing Jobless Claims
data.description:
The S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished Thursday in positive territory, although both ETFs fell from their intraday highs during the last two hours of the trading session.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a sign of a resilient labor market, initial jobless claims for the week ended January 9 decreased by 9,000 to 198,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, fell by 19,000 to 1.884 million and were also below the expectation of 1.897 million. Initial claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits.

Meanwhile, the U.S. and Taiwan have agreed to a trade deal that will see the self-governing island's tech firms commit to investing at least $250 billion into semiconductor, AI, and energy operations in the U.S. In addition, the Taiwanese government will provide a minimum of $250 billion in credit guarantees to Taiwanese companies for investing in the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem. In return, the U.S. will drop Taiwan's tariff rate to 15% from 20% and exempt generic pharmaceuticals, the ingredients used in those drugs, aircraft components, and some natural resources from duties.

Elsewhere, two Fed officials warned of steep consequences if the Fed loses its independence amid a Justice Department probe into Fed Chair Jerome Powell over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. "Anything that's infringing or attacking the independence of the central bank is a mess," Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee told CNBC's "Squawk Box." "You're going to get inflation come roaring back if you try to take away the independence of the central bank."

Furthermore, Federal Reserve Governor Michael Barr framed the investigation as an "assault on the independence of the Fed." Barr stressed that the Fed's monetary policy decisions take into account inflation and the labor market while casting aside political biases.

Finally, President Trump is considering invoking the Insurrection Act to address mass protests against Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents in Minnesota. The federal law allows the president to deploy the U.S. military domestically to help suppress unrest, insurrection, or rebellion. Earlier this week, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz accused the Trump administration of "organized brutality" after an ICE agent fatally shot 37-year-old Minneapolis resident Renee Good.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.26% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.32%.

data.author: Eddie Pan
